question_title: How far will the Duke Blue Devils advance in the NCAA's 2026 March Madness?
summary: Duke is the #1 overall seed projection (28-2, #1 AP ranking) with Cameron Boozer as Wooden Award favorite. Betting markets have Duke as championship favorite (+320, ~20% implied) with strong Final Four odds (-175, ~57% implied). The probability distribution is anchored on these two market-implied data points, with earlier rounds filled using historical 1-seed base rates scaled for Duke's above-average strength. The modal outcome is Final Four elimination (21%), reflecting that this is where top seeds most commonly encounter peer-level competition. Single-elimination tournament variance ensures meaningful probability across multiple rounds.

factors.description: Duke is projected as #1 overall seed (28-2 record, #1 AP ranking), with Cameron Boozer as Wooden Award favorite. This significantly exceeds generic 1-seed strength.
Betting markets have Duke as championship favorite at +320 (implied ~20% de-vigged), much higher than generic 1-seed rate of 16%
Duke Final Four odds at -175 (implied ~57% de-vigged), indicating strong probability of deep run
Historical 1-seed base rates: ~99% past R64, ~85% past R32, ~66% past S16, ~40% to F4, ~25% to CG, ~16% win championship
Duke reached Final Four in 2025 under Scheyer, showing program continuity and upward trajectory
March Madness inherent variance - single elimination tournament format creates significant upset risk regardless of team quality
Strong competition field with Michigan (27-2), Arizona, UConn also as 1-seeds - Duke is not clearly dominant over the field

reasoning:
**Forecast Summary:**

| Outcome | Probability |
|---|---|
| Round of 64 | 0.5% |
| Round of 32 | 9.5% |
| Sweet 16 | 16.0% |
| Elite Eight | 17.0% |
| Final Four | 21.0% |
| Championship Game (loss) | 16.0% |
| Championship Game (win) | 20.0% |

**Key reasoning:**
- Duke is the strongest team in the field (28-2, #1 overall seed projection, Wooden Award favorite Cameron Boozer)
- Market-anchored: championship odds at +320 (~20%) and Final Four odds at -175 (~57%) provide strong calibration
- Historical 1-seed base rates scaled upward to reflect Duke's above-average quality
- Modal outcome is Final Four (21%), where top seeds typically face their stiffest competition
- 36% combined probability of reaching the championship game reflects Duke's elite status while respecting single-elimination variance

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve as the furthest stage of the 2026 men's NCAA division I basketball tournament that the Duke Blue Devils qualify for.
fine_print:
If the tournament is canceled or the Duke Blue Devils do not qualify for the round of 64, this question will be annulled.

If Duke reaches the national championship game, the question will resolve according to whether Duke wins or loses that game.

However, if this question would resolve differently than the original question, then this question will be annulled.
